key_insight: PF-DIC merges phase-field fracture modeling with digital image correlation to simultaneously measure displacement, strain, and evolving damage/cracks from speckle images.
engineering_relevance: This enables more accurate full-field measurements near cracks without manual masks, supporting fracture analysis, defect characterization, and structural health monitoring.
method: Embed a phase-field damage variable into the DIC objective to represent crack evolution.
Use the PF formulation to track complex crack paths and improve displacement accuracy near crack tips.
Implement the framework with a finite element solver for full-field experimental image correlation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical examples showing PF-DIC can capture different crack types and improve displacement measurement accuracy, with selective crack identification under specific loading conditions.
watch_item: The approach adds model and implementation complexity, and practical performance will depend on reliable phase-field parameter calibration and finite element setup.
paper_title: PF-DIC: Phase field digital image correlation for integrated full-field displacement, strain, and damage measurements
